proven.claim: PROVEN: paramsNode null guard fires before loop body at line 201 is reachable
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 201
; PROVEN: paramsNode null guard fires before loop body at line 201 is reachable
(declare-const paramsNode_is_null Int)  ; 1 = childForFieldName returned null
(declare-const loop_body_reached Int)   ; 1 = execution enters for-loop body

; Code transition: early return when paramsNode is null
(assert (=> (= paramsNode_is_null 1) (= loop_body_reached 0)))
; Code transition: loop body reached only when paramsNode is non-null
(assert (=> (= paramsNode_is_null 0) (= loop_body_reached 1)))

; Negate property: loop body reached while paramsNode is null
(assert (= loop_body_reached 1))
(assert (= paramsNode_is_null 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: PROVEN: a single child cannot satisfy both if-conditions simultaneously
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 201
; PROVEN: a single child cannot satisfy both if-conditions simultaneously
(declare-const child_is_required_or_optional Int)  ; 1 = first if matches
(declare-const child_is_object_or_array Int)        ; 1 = second if matches
(declare-const push_count Int)                      ; pushes from this one child

; Code transition: each branch pushes independently
(assert (=> (= child_is_required_or_optional 1) (>= push_count 1)))
(assert (=> (= child_is_object_or_array 1) (>= push_count 2)))

; Structural invariant: a node has exactly one type — these sets are disjoint
(assert (= (+ child_is_required_or_optional child_is_object_or_array) 1))

; Negate property: push_count >= 2 from a single child
(assert (>= push_count 2))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: typeNode non-null but replace produces "" — semantic type is lost
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 201
; VIOLATION: typeNode non-null but replace produces "" — semantic type is lost
(declare-const typeNode_exists Int)          ; 1 = typeNode non-null
(declare-const typeNode_text_chars Int)      ; chars in typeNode.text
(declare-const stripped_chars Int)           ; chars after replace(/^:\s*/, "")
(declare-const output_type_is_empty Int)     ; 1 = result is ""
(declare-const output_type_is_unknown Int)   ; 1 = result is "unknown"

; Code transition: typeNode is non-null
(assert (= typeNode_exists 1))
; Code transition: typeNode.text is only a colon/whitespace prefix (len = 1 or 2)
(assert (>= typeNode_text_chars 1))
(assert (<= typeNode_text_chars 2))
; Code transition: replace(/^:\s*/, "") strips the entire content
(assert (= stripped_chars 0))
; Code transition: output is "" when stripped_chars = 0
(assert (=> (= stripped_chars 0) (= output_type_is_empty 1)))
; Code transition: "unknown" path only taken when typeNode is null
(assert (=> (= typeNode_exists 1) (= output_type_is_unknown 0)))

; Violation: type field is "" — not "unknown", not a real annotation
(assert (= output_type_is_empty 1))
(assert (= output_type_is_unknown 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: child with unrecognized type is silently dropped from params
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 201
; VIOLATION: child with unrecognized type is silently dropped from params
(declare-const child_is_required_or_optional Int)  ; 1 = first if fires
(declare-const child_is_object_or_array Int)        ; 1 = second if fires
(declare-const child_is_other_type Int)             ; 1 = neither if fires
(declare-const child_pushed Int)                    ; 1 = pushed to params

; Code transition: push only when a condition matches
(assert (=> (= child_is_required_or_optional 1) (= child_pushed 1)))
(assert (=> (= child_is_object_or_array 1) (= child_pushed 1)))
; Code transition: no else-clause — unrecognized type produces no push
(assert (=> (= child_is_other_type 1) (= child_pushed 0)))

; Code transition: node has exactly one type classification
(assert (= (+ child_is_required_or_optional child_is_object_or_array child_is_other_type) 1))

; Violation: a valid named parameter child is silently omitted from output
(assert (= child_is_other_type 1))
(assert (= child_pushed 0))
(check-sat)
